---
title: "Covid19"
output: word_document
---

```{r}
# Part A: Dataset Understanding and Preparation
# 1. Import the COVID-19 dataset
library(dplyr)

covid <- read.csv(
  "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasets/covid-19/main/data/countries-aggregated.csv"
)

# View dataset structure
str(covid)
```

```{r}
# 2. Display first 10 and last 10 records
head(covid, 10)
tail(covid, 10)
```

```{r}
# 3. Identify total rows and columns
total_rows <- nrow(covid)
total_columns <- ncol(covid)

total_rows
total_columns
```

```{r}
# 4. List variables and data types
sapply(covid, class)
```

```{r}
# 5. Convert Date column into Date format
covid$Date <- as.Date(covid$Date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
```

```{r}
# 6. Healthcare significance of variables
# Confirmed: Total reported COVID-19 cases
# Recovered: Number of patients who recovered
# Deaths: Total deaths caused by COVID-19
```

```{r}
# Part B: Data Cleaning and Preprocessing
# 1. Check for missing (NA) values
colSums(is.na(covid))
```

```{r}
# 2. Handle missing values
covid$Confirmed[is.na(covid$Confirmed)] <- 0
covid$Recovered[is.na(covid$Recovered)] <- 0
covid$Deaths[is.na(covid$Deaths)] <- 0
```

```{r}
# 3. Detect outliers using IQR method
detect_outliers <- function(x) {
  Q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  Q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  which(x < (Q1 - 1.5 * IQR) | x > (Q3 + 1.5 * IQR))
}

out_confirmed <- detect_outliers(covid$Confirmed)
out_recovered <- detect_outliers(covid$Recovered)
out_deaths <- detect_outliers(covid$Deaths)

length(out_confirmed)
length(out_recovered)
length(out_deaths)
```

```{r}
# 4. Handle outliers (capping method)
cap_outliers <- function(x) {
  Q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  Q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  lower <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  x[x < lower] <- lower
  x[x > upper] <- upper
  return(x)
}

covid$Confirmed <- cap_outliers(covid$Confirmed)
covid$Recovered <- cap_outliers(covid$Recovered)
covid$Deaths <- cap_outliers(covid$Deaths)
```

```{r}
# 5. Create cleaned dataset
covid_clean <- covid
```

```{r}
# Part C: Data Manipulation
# 1. Filter dataset for a specific country
india_data <- covid_clean %>%
  filter(Country == "India")
head(india_data,10)
```

```{r}
# 2. Extract data for year 2020
data_2020 <- covid_clean %>%
  filter(format(Date, "%Y") == "2020")
head(data_2020,10)
```

```{r}
# 3. Create new column: Active_Cases
covid_clean <- covid_clean %>%
  mutate(Active_Cases = Confirmed - Recovered - Deaths)
head(covid_clean, 10)
```

```{r}
# 4. Group by country and calculate totals
country_summary <- covid_clean %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Confirmed = max(Confirmed),
    Total_Deaths = max(Deaths),
    Total_Recovered = max(Recovered)
  )
head(country_summary, 10)
```

```{r}
# 5. Top 10 countries with highest confirmed cases
top_10_countries <- country_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Confirmed)) %>%
  head(10)

top_10_countries
```

```{r}
# Part D:Data Visualization (Using ggplot2)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Scatter Plot
# Relationship between Confirmed Cases and Deaths
ggplot(covid_clean,
       aes(x = Confirmed, y = Deaths)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkred", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Confirmed COVID-19 Cases vs Deaths (Country-wise)",
    x = "Total Confirmed Cases (log scale)",
    y = "Total Deaths (log scale)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# 2. Line Charts
# a) Trend of confirmed cases over time for a selected country (India)
india_data <- covid_clean %>% filter(Country == "India")

ggplot(india_data, aes(x = Date, y = Confirmed)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "COVID-19 Confirmed Cases Over Time (India)",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Confirmed Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# b) Compare confirmed cases for three countries
countries_data <- covid_clean %>%
  filter(Country %in% c("India", "United States", "Brazil"))

ggplot(countries_data, aes(x = Date, y = Confirmed, color = Country)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Confirmed COVID-19 Cases",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Confirmed Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# 3. Bar Charts
# a) Vertical bar chart – Top 10 countries by total deaths
 top_deaths <- covid_clean %>%
   group_by(Country) %>%
   summarise(Total_Deaths = as.numeric(sum(Deaths))) %>%
   arrange(desc(Total_Deaths)) %>%
   slice_head(n = 10)
 
 ggplot(top_deaths, aes(x = reorder(Country, -Total_Deaths), y = Total_Deaths)) +
   geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkred") +
   #scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
   labs(
     title = "Top 10 Countries by Total COVID-19 Deaths",
    x = "Country",
    y = "Total Deaths"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
 
 # b) Horizontal bar chart – Recovered cases by country (Top 10)
 top_recovered <- covid_clean %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(Total_Recovered = as.numeric(sum(Recovered))) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Recovered)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

ggplot(top_recovered, aes(x = Total_Recovered, y = reorder(Country, Total_Recovered))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkgreen") +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Countries by Recovered COVID-19 Cases",
    x = "Recovered Cases",
    y = "Country"
  ) +
   scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# 4. Histograms
# a) Histogram of Confirmed Cases
ggplot(covid_clean, aes(x = log10(Confirmed + 1))) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Log-Transformed Distribution of Confirmed COVID-19 Cases",
    x = "log10(Confirmed + 1)",
    y = "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

`{b) Interpret the histogram in terms of healthcare impact.`

`Healthcare Interpretation: The histogram shows a right-skewed distribution, indicating that most countries have low to moderate case counts, while a few countries experience extremely high case loads, stressing healthcare infrastructure.}`

```{r}
# 5. Box Plots
# a) Box plot for Confirmed Cases

library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

covid_long <- covid_clean %>%
  select(Confirmed, Deaths) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Confirmed, Deaths),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Count"
  )

ggplot(covid_long, aes(x = Type, y = log10(Count + 1), fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "COVID-19 Cases vs Deaths (log10(x + 1))",
    y = "log10(Count + 1)",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )
```
